title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Opens Today as Field's Premier Convening
description: Vitalist Bay 2026 kicks off Thursday May 14 at Berkeley's Lighthaven, running through Sunday May 17 with 100+ speakers including Aubrey de Grey, Joe Betts-LaCroix, Michael Snyder, and Morgan Levine. Six tracks (Biotech, Investors, Replacement, Longevity Science, AI x Bio, Biostasis), 50+ workshops, on-site DEXA/CGM/blood panels, and dedicated daily startup pitch sessions make this the field's most consequential capital-formation and clinical-experience event of the year.

title: University of Sydney: 4-Week Dietary Change Reduces Biological Age in 65-75 Year Olds
description: An Aging Cell paper from Dr Caitlin Andrews shows older Australians who cut dietary fat or animal protein for just four weeks register reductions in biological age via biomarker profile, with the strongest signal in the omnivorous high-carb (lower-fat) arm. The result strengthens the case that simple, scalable nutritional shifts can move age biomarkers in a clinically meaningful direction on a four-week timescale — without supplements or pharmacotherapy.

title: Biotech Sector Holds Tuesday Gains as XBI +0.70% and SBIO +1.06% Track Discovery Risk-On
description: Wednesday's tape showed selective consolidation after Tuesday's broad rally: XBI +0.70% and SBIO +1.06% led, indicating risk-on rotation into smid-cap discovery names ahead of Vitalist Bay catalysts. BBH (-0.54%) gave back part of Tuesday's surge, while large-cap healthcare (XLV +0.59%) and IBB (+0.21%) extended gains modestly — a healthier breadth pattern than Tuesday's tier-one-only rally.

title: UT San Antonio Rapamycin Trial Confirmed: 84-Participant RCT, NIA Funded
description: The much-discussed academic rapamycin trial is officially housed at UT Health San Antonio with NIA funding, comparing daily, intermittent, and placebo regimens in approximately 84 healthy older adults across a six-month treatment plus six-month follow-up window. Enrollment is now open for non-diabetic, non-smoking adults aged 65-90, marking the first rigorous, well-powered academic RCT to address rapamycin dosing for geroprotection.

title: Insilico Longevity Board With Lilly's Adams as Chair Anchors Field's Big-Pharma Pivot
description: Andrew Adams' move from Eli Lilly to chair Insilico's industry-first Longevity Board, alongside Nobel laureate Michael Levitt, signals that big pharma now views aging biology as a strategic R&D vector rather than a fringe academic pursuit. With Lilly already paying $115M upfront plus up to $2.75B in milestones to Insilico for AI-discovered orals, this governance move is the operational complement to the field's largest commercial deal.

title: Dietary Changes in Older People Can Improve Biological Age
description: Aging Cell publication from Dr Caitlin Andrews shows 65-75 year olds who reduced fat or animal-based protein for four weeks registered measurable reductions in biological age via biomarker profile.

title: Scientists Reversed Biological Age in Older Adults With 4-Week Diet Change
description: Coverage of the Sydney Aging Cell study highlights the strongest reductions in the omnivorous higher-carb / lower-fat arm, with the closest-to-baseline group showing no change.

title: Insilico Announces Industry's First Longevity Board
description: Andrew Adams (Lilly) chairs new Longevity Board with Nobel laureate Michael Levitt; mandate covers life models, biomarkers of aging, dual-purpose targets, and clinical validation of healthspan effects.

title: Human Longevity Launches $599 Clinical-Grade Whole Genome Sequencing
description: Human Longevity, Inc. unveiled a $599 clinical-grade whole genome report with AI-driven prevention insights, honoring founder J. Craig Venter's legacy.

title: Longevity News Roundup — Week 19, 2026
description: Roundup examines multi-target Alzheimer's therapy, sugar glycation in aging, personalized CRISPR for Parkinson's, FDA real-time trials, and peptide platforms.

title: ARDD 12th Meeting Report — Toward Actionable Interventions in Human Aging
description: Aging-US Volume 18 publishes the ARDD synthesis: epigenome as central regulator, partial cellular reprogramming, and systemic effects pointing to organ-wide rejuvenation.

title: Large Rapamycin Clinical Trial Launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: Multi-phase clinical study with ~84 older adults will determine optimal rapamycin dosage for healthy aging via a six-month treatment, six-month follow-up RCT against placebo and intermittent dosing.

title: Want to Reverse Your Biological Age? Change What You Eat
description: Inc. covers the Sydney study, noting older adults reducing animal-derived proteins and fats saw notable improvements in cholesterol, insulin, and blood pressure within four weeks.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Set to Spotlight Longevity's Next Phase
description: Four-day Vitalist Bay festival at Lighthaven, Berkeley brings together researchers, founders, clinicians and investors across biotechnology, AI x Bio, biostasis, and health optimization.

title: Vitalist Bay Returns: Four-Day Longevity Festival May 14-17
description: Adam Gries and Nathan Chang's bootstrapped Berkeley event packs depth across six tracks: AI x Bio, scientific findings, biopreservation, replacement, longevity science, and investors.

title: BioAge Labs R&D Day: BGE-102 Phase 1 Shows Big hsCRP Drops
description: BioAge presented Phase 1 results for BGE-102 with 24-hour coverage at 60 mg once-daily dosing and profound hsCRP reductions in obese subjects, setting up cardiovascular and DME POC studies.

title: Creatine Shows Synergy With Exercise in Older Adults
description: Spanish researchers in Experimental Gerontology showed creatine combined with power training improved BDNF, antioxidant defense, inflammation, and lower-limb function in 103 older adults over 16 weeks.

title: May 2026 Clinical Studies in Aging Roundup
description: Active studies include everolimus mTORC1 inhibition (NCT05835999), RAP PAC rapalog pharmacology (NCT05949658), and a GLP-1 trial in postmenopausal women evaluating aging biomarkers.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identify chloroacetamide senolytics that selectively kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4 and triggering ferroptosis.

title: Rubedo Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496
description: First-in-class topical GPX4 modulator was well tolerated with dose-related target engagement, senescent-cell reduction, lower inflammatory cytokines, and ~20% reduction in epidermal thickness.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Sign $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Pact
description: Lilly grants Insilico $115M upfront with up to $2.75B in milestones plus tiered royalties for exclusive worldwide license to a pipeline of oral preclinical assets.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026
description: Monthly digest: replacement-over-repair strategy, inflammaging-epigenetic clock links, engineered stem-cell antibody factories, SRN-901 lifespan extension, BCLXL-PROTAC senolytics in COPD.

title: NYT Magazine: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form feature on partial reprogramming, Altos Labs, and the first safety trial of rejuvenation therapy in humans targeting glaucoma.

title: Junyue Cao on How the Body Ages, Cell by Cell
description: Rockefeller researcher discusses ultra-high-throughput single-cell technologies applied to mapping cellular hallmarks of aging across tissues.

title: How Intestinal Aging Encourages Harmful Bacteria
description: Aging Cell study elucidates bidirectional relationship between intestinal epithelial aging and microbiome shifts that favor pathogenic species.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT dataset projects 2026 capital flows of $8-9B (55-60% YoY growth), with median deal size of $21.8M signaling a maturing pipeline.

title: Inexion: $554M Q4 Longevity Funding Yet RWE Data Gap Persists
description: Q4 2025 longevity biotech funding hit $554M (900% above Q3), but no major claims-first data platform reaches the cash-pay longevity cohort.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan 33% in Aged Mice
description: SRN-901 multipathway treatment of 18-month-old mice produced a 33% increase in median remaining lifespan plus reductions in frailty and tumor incidence.

title: Inbrain Completes Enrollment in First Human Graphene Brain Interface Study
description: First-in-human study of graphene-based neural interfaces fully enrolled, supporting future applications in age-related neurodegeneration.

title: Sleep Apnea, Intermittent Hypoxia, and p16-Mediated Senescence
description: Preprint links sleep apnea-induced intermittent hypoxia to p16Ink4a senescence in vascular endothelium, suggesting a senescence component to CPAP-resistant cardiovascular risk.
